note: Every figure is a single run, not repeated or averaged, and none of them is an isolated benchmark: the BERTMap-family ranking runs were launched in pairs, one per GPU, so a second matcher process was sharing the host's CPU and memory bandwidth throughout. Read them as orders of magnitude, not as a controlled efficiency comparison. They cover the matcher run itself — building the task splits, and the reasoner coherence checks reported above, are separate steps that are not counted here.

hardware.host: One workstation; every baseline ran locally on it.
hardware.cpu: AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 9965WX — 24 cores / 48 threads, boost to 5.49 GHz, 128 MB L3
hardware.gpu: 2 × NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Max-Q Workstation Edition, 96 GB, 300 W
hardware.storage: PCIe Gen5 NVMe SSD 1 TB system volume, 4 TB working volume

software.jvm: OpenJDK 21 — heap 256 GB for AML / LogMap / LogMapLt, 48–128 GB for the BERTMap family
software.bert: DeepOnto 0.9.3, bert-base-uncased, max sequence length 256, 3 epochs, batch 32 (training) / 256 (prediction)
software.reasoner: ELK, for BERTMap's internal mapping repair

global.label: Subtrack 1 — global equivalence alignment
global.phase: matching: producing each system's global alignment over the two full ontologies

LogMap.note: The 24-hour SNOMED–FMA cell is the outlier of the whole table; the other two pairs finish in minutes to hours.

BERTMap.note: Includes the in-run BERT fine-tune on each pair's own ontology synonyms (about an hour of the NCIT–DOID cell), which is why it is the most expensive baseline here.

global.source: AML / LogMap / LogMapLt: per-cell wall-clock recorded by the baseline runner, whose cells do nothing but match, write and score. BERTMap / BERTMapLt: the matcher wrapper's own end-of-matching log line, which excludes the ranking and Track-2 export that shared the same runner cell.

local.label: Subtrack 2 — local equivalence ranking
local.phase: ranking: scoring every candidate list in the test split

BERTMap-ss.note: Fine-tunes fresh on the public train+valid maps for every split — the fine-tune must not be shared across splits if the test firewall is to hold — which is the whole of its cost gap over BERTMap here.

BERTMap.note: Reuses the fine-tuned checkpoint from its Subtrack-1 run, so the fine-tuning cost is counted in the matching table above and not here.

local.source: Per-cell wall-clock recorded by the baseline runner for the ranking-only runs that produced the published MRR / Hits@k.
